role: A vector-lens representation picker rendered as a dropdown chip that switches between full-float and binary quantization.
capabilities: Conditionally renders only for vector lenses (proj.binding.space)
Toggles a dropdown menu via useChipMenu
Lets the user pick 'Full' or 'Coarse (binary)' representation
Calls setQuant with 'binary' or null on selection
Closes menu via scrim click or option selection
substance_why: The file implements a complete, wired dropdown control with real options, state callback, conditional visibility, animation, and telemetry stamps.
interactions_present: Click chip button to open/close menu
Click menu option to select representation
interactions_missing: aria-haspopup / aria-controls linking button to menu
No live region or other selection feedback
performance_risks: Framer-motion AnimatePresence runs on every open/close
Inline stamp() and menu item handlers recreated each render
Parent re-render propagates through setQuant on every selection
reuse_why: Keep the representation concept, option set, and semantic stamps, but rebuild the presentation and hook integration for the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Extract a generic LensChip shell from the repeated menu/scrim/button structure
Move OPTS label data and quant mapping into a config module
Isolate animation wrapper into a shared MenuPopover component
Separate stamp identifiers into a constants file
